reviews.author: Amir Goldstein
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch lacks mnt_want_write() and suggested moving heavy logic to fs/backing_file.c helpers, which could be shared between overlayfs and fuse passthrough
reviews.raw_body:
Looking at ovl_setattr() (overlayfs should be the reference to all
passthrough ops)
it looks like mnt_want_write() is missing and possibly many other tweaks

It is generally advisable to keep heavy logic as this in
fs/backing_file.c helpers,
which overlayfs and fuse can share, but I did not look to see how much of
ovl_setattr() is relevant for fuse passthrough.

Thanks,
Amir.

reviews.author: Amir Goldstein
reviews.summary: Reviewer Amir Goldstein noted that setattr is not a file operation and suggested moving the common code for setattr to fs/backing_inode.c or creating a new library, citing outdated helpers in fs/stack.c as an example.
reviews.raw_body:
Also setattr is not a file op, so if we move to common code it
should probably be fs/backing_inode.c or something (*).

Thanks,
Amir.

(*) there are a few common helpers in fs/stack.c, but there are out dated
and only used by ecryptfs, so better start a new lib.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged the need to review additional files in addition to the original patch, showing willingness to address all related code
reviews.raw_body:
Great, I will take a look at fs/backing_file.c and fs/stack.c as well.

Thanks,
Joanne

2026-04-20.patch_summary: This patch adds a passthrough setattr functionality to the FUSE file system, allowing attribute changes to be made directly on the backing inode via notify_change() instead of issuing a FUSE_SETATTR request to the server. The patch also updates the fuse inode's vfs-visible attributes with a passthrough getattr after updating the backing inode. This approach is more efficient and reduces the number of requests sent to the server, improving performance.
